diagrams.title: Application to Operating System Interaction Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchical flow of how an application program interacts with the operating system kernel through APIs and system calls
elements: Application Programming Interface (API) layer below it
System Call Library/API (such as libc) next layer
diagrams.purpose: Students learn how requests travel from user applications to the OS kernel, understanding the role of each layer in the communication process

diagrams.title: System Call Interface Architecture
diagrams.description: Block diagram showing the architecture of system call interface in an operating system
elements: Return mechanism back to user space
Arrow showing parameter passing through registers
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the internal mechanism of how system calls work, including how parameters are passed and how control transfers between user and kernel modes

diagrams.title: API Categories for Operating System Services
diagrams.description: Classification diagram showing different categories of operating system APIs
elements: Six category branches: Process Management API, Memory Management API, File Management API, Device Management API, Communication API, Protection API
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand the scope of operating system APIs and how they are categorized based on the type of service provided

diagrams.title: System Call Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing the step-by-step execution of a system call
elements: Decision: Library function or direct syscall?
diagrams.purpose: Students learn the complete lifecycle of a system call from invocation to completion, understanding the mode transition and parameter handling

charts.title: API vs System Call vs Function Call Comparison
charts.content: Table with three columns comparing: API (library functions provided to developers), System Call (kernel service request interface), Function Call (regular procedure call). Rows compare: Execution mode (user/kernel), Privileges required, Return mechanism, Examples (printf/read/strlen)

charts.title: Common System Call Categories and Examples
charts.content: Six-row table listing: Process Control (fork, exec, exit), File Management (open, read, write, close), Device Management (ioctl, read, write), Information Maintenance (getpid, alarm, sleep), Communication (pipe, shmget, msgsnd), Protection (chmod, umask, chown)

charts.title: Operating System Interface Types
charts.content: Flowchart showing three interface paths from OS to users: Command Line Interface (CLI) → Terminal, Graphical User Interface (GUI) → Windows/Icons, Application Programming Interface (API) → Programs. Each leads to different user types.

charts.title: System Call Parameter Passing Methods
charts.content: Table comparing three methods: Register method (parameters in CPU registers), Block method (parameters in memory block with address in register), Stack method (parameters pushed onto stack). Columns: Advantages, Disadvantages, Example OS

branches: Definition: Set of routines available to programmers
System Calls: Interface to kernel services
Library Functions: Wrappers around system calls
API Categories: Process, Memory, File, Device, Communication, Protection
Examples: POSIX API, Win32 API, System V API
Purpose: Abstraction layer for OS services
mindMaps.purpose: Provides comprehensive overview of API concepts in operating systems, connecting all related terms and showing hierarchical relationships between components
